tool.name: NCBI SRA AWS Fetch
tool.description: Fetches one or more SRA runs from AWS S3 and converts them to FASTQ
tool.command:
## Create a clean list of accessions from the user input echo "$accession" | sed 's/,/\n/g; s/ \+/\n/g' | grep . > accessions.txt &&
## Loop over each clean accession for acc in $(cat accessions.txt); do echo "Processing accession: $acc" &&
## 1. Create unique directories for this accession mkdir -p sra_cache_${acc} fastq_out_${acc} &&
## 2. Download the file from S3 using aws s3 cp aws s3 cp --no-sign-request "s3://sra-pub-run-odp/sra/${acc}/${acc}" ./sra_cache_${acc}/ &&
## 3. Convert with fasterq-dump fasterq-dump --outdir ./fastq_out_${acc} --temp . --threads \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4} --split-files ./sra_cache_${acc}/${acc} &&
## 4. Compress with pigz pigz -p \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4} ./fastq_out_${acc}/*.fastq &&
## 5. Move outputs for collection discovery #if str($layout) == 'paired' mv ./fastq_out_${acc}/${acc}_1.fastq.gz '$output_r1.files_path/${acc}_1.fastq.gz' && mv ./fastq_out_${acc}/${acc}_2.fastq.gz '$output_r2.files_path/${acc}_2.fastq.gz' #else mv ./fastq_out_${acc}/${acc}.fastq.gz '$output_r1.files_path/${acc}.fastq.gz' #end if &&
## 6. Clean up rm -rf sra_cache_${acc} fastq_out_${acc} done

param.name: accession
param.label: SRA Accession(s)
param.help: Provide one or more accession numbers (separated by commas, spaces, or newlines). This field accepts a dataset list of accessions in a workflow.

param.name: layout
param.label: Read layout
param.help: This setting is applied to all accessions.

tool.help:
**NCBI SRA AWS Fetch**
Fetches one or more SRA runs from the public `sra-pub-run-odp` S3 bucket and converts them to gzip-compressed FASTQ using `fasterq-dump`. This tool uses `aws s3 cp` for direct downloads within the AWS environment.

citations.citation: @article{sra_toolkit, title = {The {NCBI} {SRA} and portable data in biology}, author = {Leinonen, Rasko and Sugawara, Hideaki and Shumway, Martin and {International Nucleotide Sequence Database Collaboration}}, journal = {Nucleic Acids Research}, volume = {39}, number = {suppl\\\_1}, pages = {D19--D21}, year = {2011}, doi = {10.1093/nar/gkq1019} }
